title: Deuteronomy: Moses' Final Song
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of deuteronomy: moses' final song and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study deuteronomy: moses' final song and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.

Review question: Who buried Moses and where, and why does the text say no one knows his burial place? Answer: God himself buried Moses in the valley opposite Beth-peor, and his burial place was kept unknown to prevent his grave becoming a site of worship.
segments.correct_answer: God buried Moses in the valley opposite Beth-peor, and no one knows the location
acceptable_variants: the LORD buried him and his grave is unknown
god himself interred moses so no shrine would be built there
Moses was buried by God in an unknown place in Moab

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The long poem in Deuteronomy 32 Moses was commanded to teach Israel; it anticipates Israel's future rebellion, God's judgment, and ultimate vindication
The promise in Deuteronomy 18:15 and 34:10 that God would raise up a prophet like Moses; fulfilled ultimately in Jesus as the greater Prophet, Priest, and King
